candidates.title:
TAKE YOURSELF
OUT
candidates.completion: Do one thing this week you would usually save for company: a movie, market, class, or meal.
candidates.narration: [energetic, fast] Pick one thing you usually save for company. Go anyway. [thoughtful] This is not about proving you are independent. It is about not making someone else's availability the price of a life you want. Feeling exposed at first is normal. That is your comfort zone growing.

candidates.title:
GO TO AN EVENT
ON YOUR OWN
candidates.spoken_title: go to an event on your own
candidates.completion: Pick one event and stay for thirty minutes before deciding whether to leave.
candidates.narration: [upbeat] Pick one event, go by yourself, and give it thirty minutes. [thoughtful] Going solo is not a punishment. It changes the question from who will come with me to what might happen if I show up.

candidates.title:
SEND THE
ACTUAL INVITE
candidates.completion: Text one person a plan with a day, time, and place. No vague maybe sometime.
candidates.narration: [confident] Make the plan specific enough that the other person can answer it. [thoughtful] The risk is not sending a text. It is letting someone see that you wanted to spend time with them. That is how plans become real.

candidates.title:
WALK INTO A
NEW PLACE
candidates.spoken_title: walk into a place you have never entered
candidates.completion: Enter one cafe, store, class, or bar you have walked past before. Ask one real question.
candidates.narration: [energised] Walk in, look around, and ask one question you could not ask from the pavement. [thoughtful] You do not need to buy anything or become a regular. Opening a door is enough to interrupt the loop.

candidates.title:
BOOK THE
BEGINNER CLASS
candidates.completion: Find one beginner class and reserve a spot before you can research your way out of it.
candidates.narration: [energetic] Reserve the beginner class before you can overthink every review. [thoughtful] Being new at something is not evidence that you do not belong there. It is how you find out whether you might love it.

candidates.title:
ASK THE
QUESTION
candidates.completion: Ask someone one question you usually keep in your head because it might sound too curious.
candidates.narration: [curious, quick] Ask the question you almost edit out because it reveals you are genuinely interested. [thoughtful] You do not need a clever follow-up. Real attention is more memorable than trying to look impressive.

candidates.title:
SIT AT THE
BAR
candidates.completion: Have one drink or snack at the bar instead of taking the safest empty table in the corner.
candidates.narration: [easygoing, upbeat] Sit at the bar for one drink or snack, and let yourself be visible for a while. [thoughtful] Nothing has to happen. But small conversations do not usually travel all the way to the empty corner table.

candidates.title:
SAY YES TO
THE INVITATION
candidates.spoken_title: say yes to the invitation you almost declined
candidates.completion: Accept one safe, manageable invitation you would normally decline out of habit.
candidates.narration: [bright] Say yes to the invitation you were about to decline by reflex. [thoughtful] A more interesting life is not always a dramatic decision. Sometimes it is already asking you in.

narration.spin_line: [bright, punchy, playful, fast] Today's side quest is on the wheel.
